title: Tanker Trade in Turbulence
summary:
Main
Beijing Strikes Back with Port Fee Policy
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2025-10-27 101247.png | assets/2025-10-27_tanker-trade-in-turbulence_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_45351909ad78.png
As of October 14, 2025, the U.S. and China have implemented reciprocal port fees targeting vessels associated with the opposing nation. These fees are levied on a per-net-ton basis, determined by a vessel's identity, specifically its ownership, operation, flag, or build, rather than its cargo.

As of October 14, 2025, the U.S. and China have implemented reciprocal port fees targeting vessels associated with the opposing nation.

children.title: China's special port fee
children.summary: China's new port-fee regulation, effective 14 October 2025, marks a targeted response to escalating U.S.-China trade frictions. The measure applies to vessels with a defined U.S.
